title: Agaloma apocynifolia
taxonomy: Agaloma apocynifolia is classified within the plant family Apocynaceae. This family is a large and diverse group of plants commonly known as the dogbane or oleander family. Members of this family are often characterized by their showy flowers and milky sap. The genus Agaloma is relatively small and includes a few species native to Central and South America. Further research is needed to confirm the exact classification of Agaloma apocynifolia within the Apocynaceae family.

Its medicinal uses include the treatment of various health conditions, such as fever and digestive issues. The plant's extracts are believed to possess anti-inflammatory properties, which may help alleviate pain and swelling. Additionally, Agaloma apocynifolia is thought to have antimicrobial properties, making it a potential remedy for infections. Further research is needed to confirm the plant's medicinal uses and their effectiveness.
